# UC-10.17.1 · SonicWall Firewall Unused Access Rule Detection via Hit Counts (SonicWall)

## Description

Many SonicWall policies accrue over years; zero-hit rules may be candidates for removal or indicate logging gaps. Tracking hits proves which controls are actually exercised.

## Value

Reduces attack surface and simplifies audits by retiring stale rules while validating that critical rules see traffic.

## Implementation

Enable enhanced firewall logging. If hit counts are not in structured fields, schedule periodic CLI/API exports into a lookup `sonicwall_rule_hits.csv` and join on rule number.

## SPL

```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall" earliest=-30d@d
| where isnotnull(rule) AND match(lower(fw_action),"(?i)allow|accept|permit")
| bin _time span=1d
| stats sum(hits) as daily_hits by rule, rule_name
| eventstats sum(daily_hits) as total_hits by rule
| where total_hits=0 OR total_hits<5
| dedup rule
| table rule, rule_name, total_hits
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (hits per rule), table (rule id, name, hits, last seen), scatter (rule age vs hits).

## References

- [Splunkbase — Dell SonicWall Add-on for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6203)
- [Splunk Connect for Syslog — Dell SonicWall reference](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/SC4S/latest/)
